audit.figure_correct_answer_desc: The condition [a] is the validity/range check at the top decision. When [a]=Yes the flow proceeds to the J+K≧20 test and computes a charge; when [a]=No it sets L=-1 (error). For both quantities J and K to be in valid range so computation can proceed, [a] must be "0≦J≦20 and 0≦K≦20". The 'and' (both in range) is required, not 'or', and it cannot be 0≦J+K≦20 (which would contradict the later J+K≧20 test).
audit.derivation: Full page (page-36, 問93) and crop agree exactly. Flowchart: 開始 → diamond [a]. From [a]: No branch (label "No" on the right) → box L=-1 → joins to 終了. Yes branch (label "Yes" below the diamond) → second diamond [J＋K≧20]. From it: No → box L=3,000×J＋1,000×K → 終了; Yes → box L=0.9×(3,000×J＋1,000×K) → 終了. So [a] is the input-validity gate: Yes ⇒ perform charge computation, No ⇒ L=-1 (error sentinel). Valid input requires BOTH J and K within 0..20, i.e. 0≦J≦20 and 0≦K≦20. Evaluating choices against the figure: ア "0≦J≦20 and 0≦K≦20" matches (both must be valid). イ uses "or" — would let an out-of-range value through, wrong. ウ "0≦J＋K≦20" contradicts the very next test J＋K≧20 (Yes path needs J+K possibly ≥20), wrong. エ "J＞20 and K＞20" inverts the gate, wrong. Choice texts shown in the figure (ア 0≦J≦20 and 0≦K≦20; イ 0≦J≦20 or 0≦K≦20; ウ 0≦J＋K≦20; エ J＞20 and K＞20) match choices_jp exactly with no swap. audit.figure_correct_answer_desc: Matrix (random-number-table) authentication: since the requested coordinate positions change every time (座標位置は毎回異なる), the password acts like a one-time value. An eavesdropped password cannot be reused for impersonation because the next login asks for different coordinates. The correct statement is "盗聴されたパスワード再利用による，なりすましの防止に有効である。" ア is false (same coordinates → same numbers → same password). エ is false (the table is the secret). イ (knowledge-based) is not the intended correct statement.

The illustrative 乱数表 (5x5 grid A-E rows 1-5: row1=37,24,99,91,76; row2=77,75,64,88,52; row3=94,84,11,41,68; row4=66,61,16,35,31; row5=47,68,81,23,29) plus example coordinates B1,C2,D3,E4 → password 24644131 (B1=24, C2=64, D3=41, E4=31). The crop and full page agree, and the highlighted cells in the crop (24, 64, 41, 31) confirm the example. Choices on the page read: ア "システムが要求する乱数表の座標位置が同じでも，パスワードは毎回異なる。"; イ "知識による認証の一種である。"; ウ "盗聴されたパスワード再利用による，なりすましの防止に有効である。"; エ "乱数表を他人に渡しても安全である。" — exactly matching stored choices_jp order. The keyed answer ウ matches the figure-derived correct answer. No anti-figure swap detected; choices labeling is consistent.

audit.figure_correct_answer_desc: a = インタプリタ (interpreter), b = コンパイラ (compiler). Left diagram: ソースプログラム and データ (入力) both feed into box "a", which directly emits 結果 (出力) — source + data executed together producing results = interpreter. Right diagram: ソースプログラム feeds into box "b" which produces 目的プログラム (object program); then データ (入力) feeds the 目的プログラム to emit 結果 (出力) — source translated into object program first, then run = compiler. So a=interpreter, b=compiler.
audit.keyed_choice_text: [表] a: インタプリタ / b: コンパイラ
audit.derivation: Read both crop and full page (page-19, 問55). The figure has two sub-diagrams plus an answer table. LEFT sub-diagram: a box labeled "a" receives ソースプログラム from the left and データ from the top (arrow labeled 入力); below the box an arrow labeled 出力 points to 結果. Meaning: source program and data enter the same processor "a" together and a result is produced directly in one step — this is the INTERPRETER (インタプリタ) model. RIGHT sub-diagram: ソースプログラム → box "b" → 目的プログラム; then データ enters 目的プログラム (入力) and 結果 is produced (出力). Meaning: box "b" translates the source into an object program which is then run with data — box "b" is the COMPILER (コンパイラ). Therefore a=インタプリタ, b=コンパイラ. The answer table on the full page reads ア(a=インタプリタ,b=インタプリタ), イ(a=インタプリタ,b=コンパイラ), ウ(a=コンパイラ,b=インタプリタ), エ(a=コンパイラ,b=コンパイラ) — identical ordering to stored choices_jp, so no choice swap. The figure-correct pair (a=インタプリタ, b=コンパイラ) is exactly choices_jp["イ"], which is the keyed answer. KEY_OK.

RIGHT diagram (box "b"): ソースプログラム enters box "b"; box "b"'s arrow curves down to produce 目的プログラム (object program); THEN データ→入力 feeds into the 目的プログラム, which emits 出力→結果. Source is first translated into an object program, then run with data — this is the COMPILER (コンパイラ) model. Therefore a=インタプリタ, b=コンパイラ, which is choice イ. The full-page answer table reads exactly ア: インタプリタ/インタプリタ, イ: インタプリタ/コンパイラ, ウ: コンパイラ/インタプリタ, エ: コンパイラ/コンパイラ — identical to choices_jp ordering, so no corruption.
